deep_dive.title: Lossy Compression for Sparse Aggregation
deep_dive.abstract: We consider the problem of transmitting sparse local updates to the server in a distributed learning system. Specifically, the system consists of $n$ clients, each possessing a $k$-sparse $d$-dimensional local model, and a central server responsible for aggregating the clients' models into a global model. The goal is to characterize the tradeoff between the communication cost in the transmission from the clients to the server and the accuracy in aggregating the global model. We propose a compression scheme for sparse local models by concatenating a covering method and a sketching method. We also present a converse based on f-divergence, which strengthens the conventional Fano-type lower bounds. The proposed lower bound is tight for the frequency estimation case, that is, each coordinate takes values in a binary alphabet. For general alphabets, the proposed achievable schemes remain suboptimal relative to the converse bounds, indicating that a complete characterization of the communication-accuracy tradeoff requires further investigation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通信高效的稀疏聚合压缩
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通信高效的稀疏聚合压缩
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通信高效的稀疏聚合压缩
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a compression scheme for sparse local updates in distributed learning, combining covering and sketching methods to trade off communication cost and aggregation accuracy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对分布式学习中稀疏局部更新的传输问题，提出了一种结合覆盖方法和草图方法的压缩方案，刻画了通信成本与聚合精度之间的权衡。该方案在频率估计场景下达到紧致下界，为分布式系统中稀疏模型的高效通信提供了理论支持。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对分布式学习中稀疏局部更新的传输问题，提出了一种结合覆盖方法和草图方法的压缩方案，刻画了通信成本与聚合精度之间的权衡。该方案在频率估计场景下达到紧致下界，为分布式系统中稀疏模型的高效通信提供了理论支持。

quick_skim.title: A Spectral Theory of Normalized Corrected GNN Propagation
quick_skim.abstract: We develop a spectral theory for \emph{normalized corrected GNN propagation}. The object of study is the symmetric normalized adjacency with its degree-stationary component removed, matching the normalization used by standard GCN-style models while isolating the stationary direction most directly tied to oversmoothing. The central theoretical question is whether this corrected normalized operator preserves class-discriminative signal after many propagation layers. Our main result is a high-probability exact-recovery theorem for the binary Contextual Stochastic Block Model after \(k=O(\log n)\) propagation steps in the dense polylogarithmic regime \(p\ge C\log^B n/n\), for any fixed \(B>4\), under explicit graph-signal and feature-SNR conditions. We also establish a multi-class partial recovery theorem showing contraction toward class centers for most nodes. Synthetic and real node-classification experiments are included as empirical checks of the theory's predicted dependence on depth, graph signal, and feature noise.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: spectral theory of GNN propagation, relevant to distributed training design
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: GNN传播的谱理论，对分布式训练设计有参考价值
quick_skim.llm_evidence: GNN传播的谱理论，对分布式训练设计有参考价值
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: GNN传播的谱理论，对分布式训练设计有参考价值
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Develops spectral theory for normalized corrected GNN propagation, proving exact recovery under certain conditions, with implications for preventing oversmoothing.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对标准化校正的GNN传播，建立了完整的谱理论，证明了在稠密多对数条件下二类随机块模型的高概率精确恢复定理，揭示了该传播方式能保持类别判别信息。该工作加深了对GNN传播机制的理解，可指导分布式训练中的层数设计与通信策略。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对标准化校正的GNN传播，建立了完整的谱理论，证明了在稠密多对数条件下二类随机块模型的高概率精确恢复定理，揭示了该传播方式能保持类别判别信息。该工作加深了对GNN传播机制的理解，可指导分布式训练中的层数设计与通信策略。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 分布式GNN训练系统与GNN NAS检索, especially work that connects or combines: communication-efficient training; graph partitioning strategies for distributed GNN; graph neural architecture search; distributed training systems for graph neural networks; find papers on communication efficient graph learning systems; find papers on distributed GNN training systems with data parallel or asynchronous execution.
